en.extract:
The sabota lark (Calendulauda sabota) is a species of lark in the family Alaudidae. It is found in southern Africa in its natural habitats of dry savannah, moist savannah, and subtropical or tropical dry shrubland. It is generally sedentary but local movements occur in drier regions. The species name is derived from sebotha or sebothé, the Tswana generic name for a lark.


Taxonomy and systematics
Formerly, the Sabota lark was classified as belonging to the genus Mirafra until moved to Calendulauda in 2009. Not all authorities have followed this re-classification. Two distinctive taxa of this species are respectively known as the large-billed Sabota lark and small-billed Sabota lark. The large-billed subspecies are found in the dry to arid west and south of its range, while the small-billed subspecies are native to mesic woodlands of the north and east.


Subspecies
Nine subspecies are recognized: 

Congo Sabota lark C. s. plebeja (Cabanis, 1875) — Originally described as a separate species in the genus Alauda. Found on the Cabinda coast of north-western Angola
Benguella Sabota lark C. s. ansorgei (Sclater, WL, 1926) — Found in western Angola
Bradfield's lark C. s. naevia (Strickland, 1853) — Formerly, some authorities considered it to be a separate species in Calendulauda or Mirafra. Found in north-western Namibia. It has a larger bill than the other subspecies of C. sabota. Several other terms have been used to name this subspecies including Damaraland Sabota lark, large-billed lark (not to be confused with another species of the same name, Galerida magnirostris) and Somali fawn-coloured lark. The common name commemorates South African naturalist R. D. Bradfield.
Ovampo Sabota lark C. s. waibeli (Grote, 1922) — Found in northern Namibia and northern Botswana
C. s. herero (Roberts, 1936) — Found in southern and eastern Namibia, north-western South Africa. Confusingly, this subspecies is also sometimes referred to as Bradfield's lark.
C. s. sabota (Smith, 1836) — Found in eastern Botswana, central Zimbabwe and north-eastern South Africa
C. s. sabotoides (Roberts, 1932) — Found in central and southern Botswana, western Zimbabwe and northern South Africa
C. s. suffusca (Clancey, 1958) — Found in south-eastern Zimbabwe, southern Mozambique and eastern South Africa
C. s. bradfieldi (Roberts, 1928) — Found in central South Africa


Gallery


References


External links

Species factsheet - BirdLife International
Species text - The Atlas of Southern African Birds

{{Short description|Species of bird}} {{speciesbox | image = Sabota Lark (Calendulauda sabota) (11421288184), crop.jpg | image2 = Calendulauda sabota sabota, roep 19s, 2022-09-24 16h21, Dinokeng, a.mp3 | image2_caption = ''C. s. suffusca'' in the [[Kruger National Park|Kruger NP]] and
the call of ''C. s. sabota'' at [[Dinokeng Game Reserve|Dinokeng GR]] | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = {{cite iucn |author=BirdLife International. |year=2024 |title=''Calendulauda sabota'' |volume=2024 |article-number=e.T22717127A263895643 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2024-2.RLTS.T22717127A263895643.en |access-date=2 August 2025}} | genus = Calendulauda | species = sabota | authority = ([[Andrew Smith (zoologist)|Smith]], 1836) | subdivision_ranks = Subspecies | subdivision = See text | synonyms = * ''Mirafra sabota'' | range_map = Calendulauda sabota distribution map.png | range_map_caption = {{legend0|#008100| range}} }} The '''sabota lark''' ('''''Calendulauda sabota''''') is a species of [[lark]] in the family [[Alaudidae]]. It is found in [[southern Africa]] in its natural [[habitat]]s of dry [[savannah]], moist savannah, and subtropical or tropical dry [[shrubland]]. It is generally sedentary but local movements occur in drier regions. The species name is derived from ''sebotha'' or ''sebothé'', the [[Tswana language|Tswana]] generic name for a lark.{{cite book |last1=Chittenden |first1=H. | last2= Allan | first2= David | last3= Weiersbye | first3= Ingrid |title=Roberts geographic variation of southern African birds |year=2012 |publisher=JVBBF |location=Cape Town |isbn=978-1-920602-00-0 |page=138 |display-authors=etal}} ==Taxonomy and systematics== Formerly, the Sabota lark was classified as belonging to the genus ''[[Mirafra]]'' until moved to ''Calendulauda'' in 2009.{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/archives/taxonomy-version-2/|title=Taxonomy Version 2 « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|access-date=2016-11-17}} Not all authorities have followed this re-classification.{{Cite web|url=http://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?avibaseid=516A194976B45CE3|title=Calendulauda sabota - Avibase|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org|access-date=2016-11-18}} Two distinctive taxa of this species are respectively known as the '''large-billed Sabota lark''' and '''small-billed Sabota lark'''. The large-billed subspecies are found in the dry to arid west and south of its range, while the small-billed subspecies are native to mesic woodlands of the north and east. === Subspecies === Nine [[subspecies]] are recognized:{{Cite journal|title=IOC World Bird List 6.4|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ioc-lists/crossref|journal=IOC World Bird List Datasets|doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.6.4|doi-access=free}} * Congo Sabota lark ''C. s. plebeja'' ([[Jean Cabanis|Cabanis]], 1875) — Originally described as a separate species in the genus ''[[Alauda]]''. Found on the [[Cabinda Province|Cabinda]] coast of north-western [[Angola]] * Benguella Sabota lark ''C. s. ansorgei'' ([[William Lutley Sclater|Sclater, WL]], 1926) — Found in western Angola * Bradfield's lark ''C. s. naevia'' ([[Hugh Edwin Strickland|Strickland]], 1853) — Formerly, some authorities considered it to be a separate species in ''Calendulauda'' or ''Mirafra''. Found in north-western [[Namibia]]. It has a larger bill than the other subspecies of ''C. sabota''. Several other terms have been used to name this subspecies including Damaraland Sabota lark, large-billed lark (not to be confused with another species of the same name, ''[[Galerida magnirostris]]'') and Somali fawn-coloured lark.{{Cite web|url=https://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?lang=EN&avibaseid=DC38D1DA9277FFD8&sec=summary&ssver=1|title=''Calendulauda'' [''sabota or naevia''] - Avibase|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org|access-date=2016-11-16}} The common name commemorates South African naturalist [[R. D. Bradfield]]. * Ovampo Sabota lark ''C. s. waibeli'' ([[Hermann Grote|Grote]], 1922) — Found in northern Namibia and northern Botswana * ''C. s. herero'' ([[Austin Roberts (zoologist)|Roberts]], 1936) — Found in southern and eastern Namibia, north-western South Africa. Confusingly, this subspecies is also sometimes referred to as Bradfield's lark. * ''C. s. sabota'' ([[Andrew Smith (zoologist)|Smith]], 1836) — Found in eastern [[Botswana]], central [[Zimbabwe]] and north-eastern [[South Africa]] * ''C. s. sabotoides'' (Roberts, 1932) — Found in central and southern Botswana, western Zimbabwe and northern South Africa * ''C. s. suffusca'' ([[Phillip Clancey|Clancey]], 1958) — Found in south-eastern Zimbabwe, southern [[Mozambique]] and eastern South Africa * ''C. s. bradfieldi'' (Roberts, 1928) — Found in central South Africa ==Gallery== Sabota Lark - Etosha 0005 (16761249109).jpg|''C. s. herero'' in [[Etosha National Park]], [[Namibia]]
(large-billed) Calendulauda sabota ssp. bradfieldi, crop.jpg|''C. s. bradfieldi'' near [[Ritchie, Northern Cape|Ritchie]], [[Northern Cape|N. Cape]]
(large-billed) Sabota lark, Calendulauda sabota, at Mapungubwe National Park, Limpopo, South Africa (18126532598).jpg|''C. s. sabotoides'' at [[Mapungubwe National Park|Mapungubwe]], [[Limpopo]]
(small-billed) Sabota lark (Calendulauda sabota waibeli).jpg|cf. ''C. s. waibeli'', in Etosha National Park, Namibia
(small-billed) ==References== {{Reflist}} == External links == {{Commonscat|Calendulauda sabota}} {{Wikispecies|Calendulauda sabota}} *[https://datazone.birdlife.org/species/factsheet/sabota-lark-calendulauda-sabota Species factsheet] - BirdLife International *[http://sabap2.adu.org.za/docs/sabap1/498.pdf Species text] - ''The Atlas of Southern African Birds'' {{Taxonbar|from=Q27075404}} [[Category:Calendulauda|sabota lark]] [[Category:Birds of Southern Africa]] [[Category:Birds described in 1836|sabota lark]] [[Category:Taxonomy articles created by Polbot]] {{Alaudidae-stub}}

zh.extract:
萨博塔歌百灵（学名：Mirafra sabota），是百灵科歌百灵属的一种，分布于纳米比亚、安哥拉、南非、斯威士兰、津巴布韦、博茨瓦纳和莫桑比克。全球活动范围约为2,310,000平方千米。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
萨博塔歌百灵的平均体重约为25.5克。栖息地包括干燥的稀树草原、亚热带或热带的（低地）干燥疏灌丛、地中海型疏灌丛和亚热带或热带的（低地）干草原。


参考文献
Mirafra sabota. BirdLife.org.   [2011-06-27].
